™	STRAIGHT LINE PERSUASION
FAST TRACK MODULE 2:
THE KEYS TO BUILDING RAPPORT
In the first module of the Straight Line Fast Track system you learned that every sale is a straight line.
There’s an open and a close and your job as a salesperson is to move the prospect down the line
while staying within the boundaries so that you control the sale.
Let’s review the three important steps of every sale…
THE 3 TENETS OF STRAIGHT LINE PERSUASION
1 	You must control the linguistic encounter—you must control the sale.
2 	Once you are in control, you establish and maintain rapport. This must be on both a
conscious and unconscious level. The prospect must believe you are a person worth
listening to and you can help them achieve their goals.
3 	You then use that rapport to gather intelligence.
YOU MASSIVE
WEALTH AND
SUCCESS
THE STRAIGHT LINE
Develop Rapport
Gather Intelligence

-- 3 of 10 --

JORDAN BELFORT
© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 4
THE TRUE MEANING OF RAPPORT
What is Rapport? Rapport, in its most basic definition, occurs when a prospect feels a connection with
you. There are two types of rapport, conscious and unconscious. You establish conscious rapport
when a person logically begins thinking that the salesperson is trustworthy and knows what they are
talking about. Whereas, you establish unconscious rapport when, for some illogical and inexplicable
reason, the prospect likes you or connects with you. Remember, people want to do business and
associate with people who are like them.
Now that you understand the true meaning of rapport, the next step is to learn how to establish and
maintain it effectively. The things you might believe are gaining rapport could actually be doing the
opposite—repulsing your prospects.
Techniques to establish rapport
• 	Matching and Mirroring is when the salesman adopts the same physiology, body language,
tonality and breathing as the prospect.
• 	Pace Pace Lead Method is when the salesman enters a prospect’s world where they are and
acts that same way for two or three beats and then moves them in the direction they want them
to go.
QUICK TIPS FOR ESTABLISHING RAPPORT WHEN IN PERSON
• 	Have no facial hair or keep it well trimmed
• 	Do not wear a lot of jewelry
• 	Men should not wear pinky rings
• 	If you are a male salesperson with a male prospect—corner off—stand to the side and
keep your hands below the waist
• 	Women like a male to face them directly with their hands above the waist
• 	If it is a woman to woman interaction—corner off—stand to the side and keep your hands
below the waist
• 	Do not be a space invader—there should be three feet of space between you and
the prospect
• 	Use the Cooperator’s Handshake—returning the pressure you get from the prospect and
match it
• 	Maintain eye contact most of the time, around 70% is ideal
• 	Be an active listener—nod your head and make sounds

-- 4 of 10 --

© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 5
™	STRAIGHT LINE PERSUASION
Remember: Appearance does matter in sales and is another way of establishing rapport—it is called
“wrapping the package.” You are creating a positive perception that you are a professional expert by
the way you present yourself.
CREATING THE ULTIMATE LEVERAGE
As you establish and maintain rapport, you then use this to gather intelligence. The main purpose of
gathering intelligence is so you can identify key details about the prospect’s values, beliefs, desires,
and pain points. Having this critical knowledge allows you to design your solution (the close of your
product or service) to meet their needs at the highest level.
TIP
• 	Dig deeper! Keep asking the prospect questions so you can identify what motivates them
and, most importantly, what their pain points are. If your product or service can get rid of
a pain point, then you have found the ultimate leverage to transition to the close.
• 	Be an active listener—your prospect should be doing most of the talking.
• 	Resist the urge to start selling—you can’t gather intelligence and sell at the same time.
Realize where you are on along the straight line and resist the urge to jump ahead. Keep
your powder dry!
COMMON MISCONCEPTION:
You do not gain rapport by making small talk about liking the same thing (i.e. “I like running or
fishing too.”). This will actually repulse the prospect because you are showing them you are
not an expert and not worth listening to. Every word of the conversation must be deliberate
in progressing along the line. Small talk is considered meaningless, as it is outside the
boundaries (Pluto!). It does not have anything to do with the product or service you are selling.

-- 5 of 10 --

JORDAN BELFORT
© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 6
THE PERFECT TRANSITION
Once you establish the initial rapport and
gather intelligence by asking great questions,
you’re ready to transition to the main body of
the presentation.
The transition is moving them into the resolution
phase of the sale—or the main body of the
presentation. This must be done with ease and
elegance so you are continuing to build rapport
and gather intelligence without losing any trust.
The main body of the presentation is where you
resolve their pain and fulfill a need.
THE STRAIGHT LINE: 5 ELEMENTS OF INFLUENCE
Fundamentally, sales is about the transfer of emotion. The straight-line method is about taking your
prospect from one emotional state to the next, or more importantly, transitioning them from a state of
uncertainty to absolute certainty.
The Continuum of Certainty = the ultimate transference of emotion
Your goal as an influencer is to move the prospect along the continuum of certainty.
The Three 10s
CAUTION: Remember to use
your straight-line ethics: If
the prospect does not qualify,
meaning if at anytime time
throughout the interaction you know the
prospect should not buy your product or
service, transition them out of the sales
funnel. Not everyone should be moved
down the straight line to a presentation.
TIP
Use the perfect transition phrase every time.
“Well, based on everything you just told me, it sounds like this is a perfect fit for you.”
10 	10	10
ELEMENT #1
FIRST 10
They love
your product
ELEMENT #2
SECOND 10
They trust and
connect with you.
ELEMENT #3
THIRD 10
They trust and
connect with your
company.

-- 6 of 10 --

© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 7
™	STRAIGHT LINE PERSUASION
To close the sale, you need to line up five specific elements that are all designed to move the
prospect from uncertainty to certainty:
Every successful sale has one thing in common—at a single moment in time, all three of these
elements are aligned and the prospect has absolute certainty in each area both logically and
emotionally, regardless of any other objections.
Once you have brought the prospect to total certainty on the first three elements, you can transition to
the close easily.
However, there are two additional elements are crucial in being able to close anyone who is
closeable. These elements are important to be aware of because they affect your ability to create
certainty as with the three 10s.
Element #4: The Action Threshold
The Action Threshold is an unconscious set point a person has in order to take action. It is a
representation of the buyer’s belief systems. Essentially, it is the level of certainty they need before
they say, “Yes!”
The action threshold is different for every individual. A higher action threshold means it will be harder
for the prospect to say ‘yes.’ A person with a lower action threshold, requires less certainty to take
action, so it will be easier to get this person to say ‘yes.’
The action threshold, however, is malleable. Your job as a salesperson is to lower the action threshold
so it will be easier for the prospect to say, “Yes.” This can be done through logical reasoning and
language patterns that produce certainty.
Ultimately, the action threshold is lowered through the use of the next element.
Element #5: The Pain Threshold
The Pain Threshold is how much pain the person feels at the moment of sale.
In order to lower somebody’s action threshold, you must raise his or her pain threshold. You can use
their own pain to empower them to make a logical and emotional buying decision, in essence to lower
their action threshold. The key to this is understanding how much pain they need before they will say
‘yes’ and take action. Your goal is to uncover this pain for your prospect, and when necessary amplify
it to get them to the point of action.
TIP
Begin with the product, once you know the prospect has absolute certainty in this area, ensure
they have absolute certainty with you, and finally the trust and connection with your company.

-- 7 of 10 --

JORDAN BELFORT
© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 8
ACTION PLAN
STEP 1: RAPPORT CARD
As you learned, both conscious and unconscious rapport is used from the beginning through the
close of a sale. So, it is important to make sure you are connecting and attracting through rapport
rather than repulsing.
What are some ways you are successful at creating rapport with your prospects?
Consciously
Unconsciously
What are some ways you thought you were developing rapport but you might have been
doing the opposite (i.e. taking the prospect off into Pluto)?
1
2

-- 8 of 10 --

© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 9
™	STRAIGHT LINE PERSUASION
What are some areas of improvement? Is it in the way you present yourself? How do you
initially engage with a prospect? When you get off track, how do you recover using rapport?
STEP 2: NO PAIN, NO GAIN
The pain and action thresholds are critical concepts in being able to motivate the prospect to say,
“Yes.” As we learned from this module, the more pain someone feels about an area of his or her life,
the lower the action threshold—or set point—where they will feel motivated to take action.
Imagine the following scenario and then answer a few questions below to discover the power of the
action and pain thresholds.
Let’s look at how you might respond to this scenario using the principles of Action Threshold and
Pain Threshold.
On a 0-10 scale, where is your Action Threshold? 0 is a low threshold where you are ready to
take immediate action (and likely submit an offer closer to the asking price). 10 is a high threshold
where you are more likely to submit a lower offer and hope for the best. How would you rate your
current Action Threshold? ___________
On a 0-10 scale, with 0 being no pain and 10 being massive pain, what would you rate your
pain level when it comes to needing a new home for your family? This is your Pain Threshold.
_________
Now, let’s see if your Action Threshold or Pain Threshold shift in the below scenario.
3
You are looking to purchase a home for your family in a very competitive real estate market.
You find your dream home, but the asking price for the house is out of your price range. You
decide you want to take advantage.
You are waiting to hear if the seller decides to counter or accept your offer, when you hear that
someone else has submitted a similar offer on the home you intend to purchase. You are now
able to submit one final counter offer to secure the sale.

-- 9 of 10 --

JORDAN BELFORT
© 2014 Global Motivation Inc. All rights reserved. 10
On a 0-10 scale, how has your Action Threshold changed? Has it lowered, i.e. are you more
likely to submit a higher counter offer as you don’t want to lose the house? Or is it still high
(where you’ll counter with an offer you still know to be lower than the buyer might be likely to
accept)? What is your Action Threshold now that there is another competing offer on the table?
_______________________
What would you rate your Pain Threshold (0 = low, 10 = high) on submitting the final counter
offer? Remember, this is your dream home! _________
It is likely you would have a much higher Pain Threshold with the counter offer due to the fact that
you could potentially lose your dream home, therefore you would most likely submit a higher offer
than originally intended—or have a lower Action Threshold.
Think about a scenario where this was true for you. How did your Action Threshold change as a
result? How will this be beneficial in your interaction with prospects?

-- 10 of 10 --
